full_prompt: Design a single-piece basswood miniature bridge for a structural efficiency (load-to-mass) design challenge. The bridge must be made from basswood (Tilia spp.) only, with cyanoacrylate adhesive. The bridge mass must not exceed 25.0 g. Overall geometric envelope: length 35.0 cm, width <= 8.0 cm, total height <= 15.0 cm, with a clear roadbed span of 30.0 cm across a 4.0 cm vertical abutment drop. The bridge bears a central load plate attached by a removable eye-bolt through the deck at the mid-span. Material: basswood, E_longitudinal = 10 GPa, compressive strength parallel to grain = 30 MPa, tensile strength parallel to grain = 60 MPa, shear strength = 4.0 MPa, density = 400 kg/m^3. Load case: a single vertical downward load is applied at mid-span through the eye-bolt and ramped until failure. Scoring: structural efficiency = load_at_failure / bridge_mass (so design target is both high failure load and low mass). Hard targets for pass/fail: (R1) Mass <= 25.0 g. (R2) Must survive a service-load check of 50 N applied at mid-span without visible cracking or fiber tear-out; max stress in any member <= compressive strength / 2.0 = 15 MPa (buckling check included). (R3) Ultimate failure load at mid-span (predicted from FEA with basswood strengths) >= 400 N; structural efficiency (failure load / bridge mass) target >= 16,000 (N/kg) equivalent to 16 N per gram. (R4) Midspan deflection at 50 N service load <= L/200 = 300 / 200 = 1.5 mm. (R5) Linear buckling eigenvalue of slender compression members at 50 N service load >= 3.0 to rule out premature Euler buckling. Primary verification: linear static + linear buckling FEA (beam or solid), plus a Tsai-Hill-like check for wood (no standard allowables in FEA, apply compressive / tensile strengths separately). Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 (or member schedule and geometry), and a report listing bridge mass in g, predicted failure load in N, efficiency ratio, midspan deflection at 50 N, first buckling factor.
short_prompt:
Design a basswood miniature bridge for a structural-efficiency (load-to-mass) design challenge. Materials restricted to basswood and cyanoacrylate adhesive. Central load is a vertical downward force applied at mid-span through a removable eye-bolt in the deck, ramped until failure; scoring is failure load divided by bridge mass.

Envelope: length 35.0 cm, width <= 8.0 cm, height <= 15.0 cm, clear roadbed span 30.0 cm across a 4.0 cm vertical abutment drop; bridge mass <= 25.0 g.

Load cases.
- LC1 service check: 50 N at mid-span with no visible cracking or fiber tear-out; mid-span deflection <= L/200 = 1.5 mm.
- LC2 ultimate: predicted failure load at mid-span >= 400 N; target structural efficiency >= 16,000 N/kg (16 N per gram).
- LC3 linear buckling: slender compression members at the 50 N service load, eigenvalue >= 3.0 to rule out premature Euler buckling.

prompt.title: Basswood miniature bridge (structural efficiency challenge)
prompt.brief: Basswood-only miniature bridge, 300 mm clear span, mass <= 25 g, aim for failure load >= 400 N.

boundary_conditions.supports: pin and roller on the two abutments separated by 300 mm

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam (B31) + shell or solid for deck
